programmes.show_name: The Story Of British Cinema: The Pioneer Years
programmes.show_description: Learn more about the boom in British cinema as new, pioneering techniques saw productivity skyrocket and creativity flourish within the industry.
In this special, the last nine winners return to the Battersea Arts Centre to paint one of Britain's best-loved personalities - in the space of just four hours.
Barbara Kingsolver and Fats Timbo: Pulitzer Prize winner Barbara Kingsolver and comedian-turned-author Fats Timbo discuss their work and influences at the 2023 Hay Festival. (S1, ep 2)
This evocative and unique film features the only TV interview ever given by Laurie Lee, including the amazing story of how it was secured by local TV producer David Parker.

programmes.show_name: Inside Art: Glyn Philpot At Pallant House
programmes.show_description: A portraitist famed for shaking off convention in the name of modernism, catch a glimpse of the first major exhibition of British artist Glyn Philpot.
Art expert Kate Bryan presents a behind the scenes look at a major exhibition of Linda McCartney's iconic photography at the Walker Art Gallery in Liverpool.
Darbar Festival 2025 20th Anniversary: Discover the beauty of ragas and talas, unwind with wellbeing sessions and immerse yourself in the meditative power of Indian music. (S1, ep 1)
India's Vanishing Voice: A documentary tracing the life of Ustad F. Wasifuddin Dagar, the last great torchbearer of the fabled Dagarvani tradition of Dhrupad singing. (S1, ep 1)
In this art competition, amateur watercolour painters compete against the clock and each other to capture a scene in just four hours. (S2, ep 29)
In this art competition, amateur watercolour painters compete against the clock and each other to capture a scene in just four hours. (S2, ep 30)
Would You Believe It: Two archaeologists working in Jordan get more than they bargain for when they decide to smuggle the statue of a mysterious woman over the border to Israel. (S4, ep 1)
Vicious Circle: When an elderly lady catches a burglar trying to rob her, she decides to help him go straight. But she has to take a different approach when he refuses to change. (S4, ep 2)
Nightmare in 4-D: A man who reads too much pulp fiction finds himself playing Sir Galahad to the blonde in the apartment downstairs. It seems she's burdened with a corpse. (S2, ep 16)
My Brother, Richard: District attorney Martin Ross is running for governor and learns that his brother Richard has killed the other candidate Burton Reeves. (S2, ep 17)
The life and career of the Academy Award-winning director, known for his 1973 horror film The Exorcist and his action-thriller The French Connection. (S6, ep 4)
A glimpse into the life and career of the Academy Award-winning Hollywood actor who starred in The Goodbye Girl, Jaws and Down and Out in Beverly Hills. (S11, ep 5)
The Boy Who Talked With Animals: When David rescues a giant turtle, it looks as though he's found a new friend. But there could be more to his pet than meets the eye. (S4, ep 3)
Best of Everything: Desperate to win the affections of his boss's daughter, Arthur accepts the help of his friend Charlie. But is he ready to count the true cost of love? (S4, ep 4)
The Great Escape: The Sack of Rome in 1527 forced many Italian artists to flee abroad, taking the revolutionary art movement with them. (3 of 3)

programmes.show_name: Reinventing The Orchestra With Charles Hazlewood
programmes.show_description: Charles explores the concept of orchestral teamwork through Mozart and his 40th symphony - showcasing how composers utilise a team to full effect. (1 of 6)
A look at the life and career of the director of Zero Dark Thirty and The Hurt Locker, the latter of which she won the Oscar for Best Director. (S6, ep 5)
A look at the life and career of the Oscar-nominated actor, known for his star turn as Sherlock Holmes and roles in David Copperfield and The Adventures of Robin Hood. (S11, ep 6)
Woman's Help: Henpecked Arthur decides to get rid of his demanding wife once and for all, so long as he gets her money. Unfortunately, his wife has a few secrets of her own... (S4, ep 5)
Shatterproof: Ellen is trapped in a loveless marriage with her wealthy husband. But her plan to kill him backfires when the hitman she hires becomes friendly with the target. (S4, ep 6)
The Manacled: Shackled prisoner Stephen Fontaine tries to negotiate an escape from Sergeant Rockwell while en route to San Quentin. (S2, 18)
A Bottle of Wine: Judge Connors shares a civilized conversation with the young architect about to steal his wife. But the quality of his mercy is strained. (S2, ep 19)
New. The Birth of Venus: Dive into Botticelli's Birth of Venus, uncovering its mythic roots, artistic brilliance and lasting power as an icon of beauty and imagination. (S1, ep 5)
New. The Last Supper: Explore the genius behind Da Vinci's Last Supper, from its bold perspective to the mysteries and theories that keep this masterpiece legendary. (S1, ep 6)
In this brand-new encounter with one of our greatest living artists, art historian and curator Kate Bryan immerses herself in icon Maggi Hambling's life and work.
New. Mainstream rock and the black middle class eye rap's rebellion warily, but its bold sound, style and energy captivate a new generation. With strong language. (S1, ep 3)

programmes.show_name: The Specials Live From Coventry Cathedral
programmes.show_description: Performing live from Coventry Cathedral Ruins in 2019, The Specials celebrate their 40th anniversary with their biggest hits, from Ghost Town to Nite Klub.
Culture Club emerged in the 80s UK new romantic scene with iconic style and music. Explore their journey and influence, highlighting the unexpected romance within the band. 2025.
Kate Mosse and Emma Dabiri: At the 2023 Hay Festival, authors Kate Mosse and Emma Dabiri sit down with Simon Savidge to discuss their books, lives and inspirations. (S1, ep 3)
Andi Oliver and Elizabeth Day host as members of Idle Readers book club from Manchester meet Ian Rankin. Plus, Delia Owens Zooms in from across the pond. (4 of 6)

programmes.show_name: Inside Art: Lubaina Himid At Tate Modern
programmes.show_description: Kate Bryan visits the studio of the artist Lubaina Himid, winner of the 2017 Turner Prize, before exploring her new large-scale exhibition at Tate Modern.

programmes.show_name: Inside Art: Night Fever At V&A Dundee
programmes.show_description: Kate Bryan explores a new exhibition that explores the relationship between club culture and design traversing the globe and spanning the 1960s to the present day.
Candoco Dance Company: A series of fascinating shorts taking you deeper into the world of art and the people behind it.

programmes.show_name: Cirque Du Soleil: Kurios - Cabinet of Curiosities
programmes.show_description: Discover a world of wonders in the Cabinet of Curiosities, a steampunk-inspired spectacle that asks you to close our eyes and trust your imagination.
